introduction.title: Monsters: The Canyon Bites Back
introduction.tagline: Not a faction. An ecology with teeth.
introduction.description: The canyon was here first. Monsters are not an army—they are the reason Coffin Canyon exists. Native inhabitants who react to intruders with instinct, territory, and survival.
introduction.philosophy: The canyon is older than your plans. Monsters do not agree to your rules. Some can be understood. Some only want you gone.
introduction.history: After the Catastrophe, the canyon’s hidden layers woke up. Tainted ground drew hungry things. Haunted ground gathered the bloodless. Strangewild growth fed predators. Every extraction, every fire, every desperate camp—another invitation. The Monsters faction represents what happens when the canyon gets a vote.

what_they_fight_for: Protection of nests, warrens, and lairs
what_they_fight_against: Anyone who thinks the canyon belongs to them

reputation.allies_see_them_as: A volatile third party—useful chaos if you can survive it.
reputation.enemies_see_them_as: A constant threat and a shifting nightmare: too many kinds, too many rules.

faction_mechanics.signature_ability_description: Monsters are built to pressure scenarios: fast nuisances, ambushers, terror auras, and heavy bruisers. Some are Befriend-eligible and become scenario pieces (escorts, calms, redirects). Others are hard-locked as hostile threats that demand response.
faction_mechanics.playstyle: A toolbox of threats: cheap objective pests, stealth assassins, terror pieces, durable anchors, and occasional Titans. Monsters excel at disrupting plans, punishing overextension, and forcing Morale tests and positional mistakes.
strengths: Wide spread of roles and stat profiles
High ceiling with ambush and terrain play
Titans and large threats can swing games
weaknesses: Many units are CannotBeBefriended (no diplomacy angle)
Can struggle vs disciplined ranged gunlines
Some units are fragile and rely on timing/cover
Faction identity is threat-based, not objective-based by default

universal_upgrades_note: Monsters typically do not use equipment upgrades. They are wild creatures, not soldiers. However, individual Monsters may have scenario-specific upgrades, mutations, or escalations.

preferred_states.reason: Predators thrive, nests multiply, and the canyon grows teeth.
Bloodless and spirits become common, and the living make worse decisions.
Toxic weather and tainted ground favor the things that were built for it.

neutral_states.reason: Chaos invites monsters, but chaos also brings more guns.
Containment reduces roaming threats, but lairs still exist.

opposed_states.reason: Order, suppression, and industrial discipline reduce monster roaming and leverage.
Strip-mined wastelands remove the ecosystem—some monsters starve out, others escalate into pure violence.
Machine dominance and dead zones reduce living ecology (Bloodless may remain, but beasts suffer).

faction_features.name: Native Deployment
faction_features.effect: Monsters deploy after all terrain is placed, but before other factions. They may deploy anywhere on the table, including inside ruins, near Thyr crystals, or in ambush positions.

faction_features.name: Monster Exits
faction_features.effect: Scenarios may designate Monster Exits (mines, caves, ruins, sewers, hidden paths). A Monster may leave the table via an Exit instead of activating normally. Exited Monsters are removed from play and may score Survival or Escape VP (scenario dependent).

faction_features.name: Temperament System
faction_features.effect: Every Monster has a Temperament (Friendly, Vengeful, Cunning, Territorial, Skittish, Stalwart) that affects how it reacts to danger and morale. See individual unit cards for specific behaviors.

faction_features.name: Befriending
faction_features.effect: Certain Monsters (marked CanBeBefriended) may be befriended, escorted, or temporarily allied with Monster Rangers. Others (marked CannotBeBefriended) react violently to such attempts.

victory_objectives.description: Monsters score VP for remaining alive at game end. Specific VP values are scenario-dependent.
Monsters score VP for remaining near nests, ruins, lairs, or designated territories. Territorial Monsters often defend specific areas.
Monsters score VP for holding or consuming Thyr crystals. Some Monsters are drawn to Thyr and gain bonuses when near it.
Monsters score VP for successfully exiting the table via Monster Exits. Represents survival and retreat rather than conquest.
Only certain predatory Monsters score VP for kills. Most Monsters do not score for combat.
Some Monsters lose points if killed, grant points to killers, or cause global VP shifts when destroyed (especially Titans).

composition_requirements.note: Monsters are scenario-driven and do not follow standard army composition rules. Scenarios determine which Monsters appear, how many, and their victory conditions.
composition_requirements.deployment_order: Monsters deploy after terrain placement but before other factions
composition_requirements.titan_rule: Titans are scenario events, not standard units. When a Titan enters play, all factions must reassess priorities.

weapons_note: Monster costs assume natural weapons and profile abilities. Weapon properties are listed when relevant (Thrown, Poisoned, etc.). Monster abilities should reference the defined ability dictionaries wherever possible.

name: Bête Herdbeast
lore: Huge, shaggy horned beasts that smell faintly of candy. Bêtes are dream-made giants—often gentle—yet terrifying when startled.
tactics: Anchor the center. Use its footprint to block lanes and protect Thyr. Avoid unnecessary fights; win by surviving, holding ground, or being Escorted.

supplemental_abilities.name: Gentle Giant
supplemental_abilities.effect: Will not declare attacks against Monster Rangers unless attacked first.

supplemental_abilities.name: Stampede
supplemental_abilities.effect: If this unit fails a Morale test it immediately moves up to its Move in a straight line. It may pass through smaller models, applying Impact normally.

supplemental_abilities.name: Pack Protector
supplemental_abilities.effect: When a Baby Bête within 6" is targeted by an attack, Mama Bête may immediately move up to 4" toward the attacker. This movement ignores engagement but must end closer to the attacker.

name: Dust King
lore: Ancient bandaged dead-men, risen from forbidden chemical ruins. Dust Kings wander out of collapsed tombs and sunken chambers, chewing fresh flesh when they find it.
tactics: Slow, durable melee pressure. Park them in ruins or choke points and force enemies to deal with Fear and grinding attacks.

supplemental_abilities.name: Ruinbound
supplemental_abilities.effect: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.

supplemental_abilities.name: Ruinbound
supplemental_abilities.effect: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.

supplemental_abilities.name: Tomb-Curse
supplemental_abilities.effect: When an enemy ends its activation engaged with this unit, that enemy must test Morale.

supplemental_abilities.name: Ruinbound
supplemental_abilities.effect: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.

supplemental_abilities.name: Royal Tomb-Ward
supplemental_abilities.effect: Once per game: choose one Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain within 12". Until end of round, enemies treat that terrain as Hazardous.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dust Command
supplemental_abilities.effect: Once per round, when a friendly Bloodless unit within 8" fails a Morale test, it may reroll.

upgrades.name: Thyr Frenzy
upgrades.effect: This unit gains Thyr Devourer and Thyr Frenzy. If this would increase its Quality above 6, it remains 6.

name: Halloween Golem
lore: Obsolete mechanical pumpkin-headed men built to bring order to the wilderness — cutting trees and tending farms. Now they wander about looking for trees to accost, and Thyr.
tactics: Reliable blockers that grind forward and absorb punishment while leaving salvage behind.
